page: The UpCloud 64xCPU-384GB is a general-purpose virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The configuration delivers 64 physical cores and 64 vCPUs with a single thread per core on a shared CPU allocation model. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing 6.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 2048 GB of bundled local SSD storage along with one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks reveal a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core CPU performance under stress-ng is relatively weak, placing in the bottom 10% of tested systems. However, multi-core performance scales effectively, achieving average marks in stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core tests. The server demonstrates strong capabilities in parallel processing, with top 25% results in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Geekbench ray tracing, and Passmark CPU tests, including physics, encryption, and extended instructions. Memory write bandwidth for large blocks is also strong, though read bandwidth remains average.

This hardware profile is well-suited for highly parallelized workloads that can leverage the 64-core architecture, such as multi-threaded decompression, ray tracing, and high-throughput database operations, as evidenced by its strong Redis SET performance of 72.18 million operations per second. Conversely, it is less optimal for single-threaded tasks or low-latency sequential processing due to its lower single-core clock speed. The bundled 2 TB SSD and high resource density offer a balanced, cost-efficient footprint for large-scale general-purpose applications and database hosting without requiring dedicated GPU acceleration.
description: The UpCloud 64xCPU-384GB is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor at 2.0 GHz, it provides 64 physical cores on a shared allocation model, paired with 384.0 GB of RAM and 2048 GB of bundled local SSD storage. While single-core benchmark performance is limited, the server excels in highly parallelized tasks, demonstrating strong multi-threaded capabilities in Passmark CPU tests, ray tracing, and bzip3 decompression. It also delivers strong memory write bandwidth and high-throughput Redis SET operations. This resource configuration makes the server highly effective for parallel processing, database hosting, and general-purpose enterprise workloads that benefit from high core counts and substantial memory capacity, though it is less suited for single-threaded applications.
og_description: UpCloud 64xCPU-384GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 64 AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB RAM, and 2048 GB of local SSD storage, optimized for highly parallelized workloads and database operations.
meta_description: UpCloud 64xCPU-384GB is a KVM virtual server with 64 AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB RAM, and 2 TB SSD storage, built for parallel workloads.
tagline: A high-core virtual server featuring AMD EPYC processors and bundled SSD storage, optimized for parallel processing and database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 AMD EPYC physical cores on an x86_64 KVM architecture
384 GB of system memory and 2048 GB of bundled local SSD storage
Strong multi-threaded performance in compression and ray tracing benchmarks
High-throughput database capabilities with strong Redis SET performance
Best suited for parallelized enterprise workloads and database hosting
Lower single-core performance makes it less ideal for single-threaded tasks
